fields.title: Fix ephemeral sandbox state notice loss between tool calls
fields.description:
The context hook in sandbox/index.ts modifies the LLM payload ephemerally (not persisted to DB). After injecting SANDBOX_STATE/SANDBOX_CHANGE, it immediately clears pendingSandboxNotice and sets lastCommunicatedHash. On the next LLM generation within the same turn (after a tool call), the notice is stripped from context. The agent loses sandbox awareness after its first generation step in a turn.

This affects both main agent and subagents.

The fix needs to ensure the sandbox state notice persists across all generation steps within a turn, not just the first one. Options:
- Re-inject on every context hook invocation (always prepend current state)
- Persist the injection into the message DB
- Keep pendingSandboxNotice alive until the turn ends

Key files:
- extensions/tau/src/sandbox/index.ts (lines 1111-1150, context hook)
- extensions/tau/src/sandbox/sandbox-change.ts (injectSandboxNoticeIntoMessages)

Run npm run gate in extensions/tau/ when done.
fields.notes: Implemented sandbox context hook to always inject current SANDBOX_STATE/SANDBOX_CHANGE notice on every invocation, keyed by hash change detection. Updated sandbox notice injection to strip existing SANDBOX_STATE/SANDBOX_CHANGE prefixes before re-injecting for idempotency. Added unit tests in extensions/tau/test/sandbox-change.test.ts. Ran npm run gate in extensions/tau: typecheck and lint passed (warnings only), tests failed due unrelated prompt-modes-session-start regressions; tracked in tau-6lk.
